join_order.note: fused scan: filter + collect l_suppkey + l_orderkey + l_quantity + l_extendedprice + l_discount
25 entries loaded at startup into array; direct index, no hash needed

aggregation_detail.note: 25 nations x 7 years = 175 groups max; direct flat array indexed by encoded key

year_extraction.note: binary search on 7 boundaries -> O(1) effective; or direct threshold chain

data_structures.part_filter: bitset(2000000) — 250KB, fits entirely in L3 cache; O(1) bit-test per lineitem row
data_structures.partsupp_join: pre_built_mmap_composite_hash — zero build cost; 16-byte entries, capacity 16777216
data_structures.supplier_join: pre_built_mmap_hash — zero build cost; 8-byte entries, capacity 262144
data_structures.orders_join: pre_built_mmap_hash — zero build cost; 8-byte entries, capacity 33554432
data_structures.nation_lookup: direct_array[25] — loaded at startup from n_name_lookup.txt
data_structures.aggregation: flat_array[200] of double — direct index by (nation_idx*8 + year_offset), thread-local copies merged after parallel scan

parallelism.note: morsel = 44MB_L3 / 64_threads / 6_columns ≈ 11K rows; use 64K for better amortization of index lookup overhead
parallelism.part_scan: single_threaded (110MB p_name + 8MB p_partkey; bitset build is sequential)
parallelism.lineitem_scan: parallel_64_threads_omp; each thread scans its morsel, probes bitset, does 3 index lookups, accumulates thread-local agg array
parallelism.synchronization: thread_local_flat_array_merge — after parallel scan, sequentially sum 64 thread-local agg arrays (175 entries each, ~trivial cost)
parallelism.index_lookup_notes: partsupp/supplier/orders indexes are read-only mmapped; concurrent reads safe without locks

index_usage.purpose: lookup ps_supplycost for each of 3.26M qualifying lineitem rows; zero build cost via mmap
lookup s_nationkey (0-24) for each qualifying lineitem row; zero build cost via mmap
lookup o_orderdate -> extract year for GROUP BY; zero build cost via mmap

scan_detail.part: single_pass: read p_name.bin (110MB) and p_partkey.bin (8MB) together; for each row, memchr for 'g' then memmem for 'green'; if match, set bit in bitset
scan_detail.lineitem: single_pass_fused_parallel: read l_partkey first for filtering, then l_suppkey + l_orderkey + l_quantity + l_extendedprice + l_discount for qualifying rows only; all 6 columns scanned in one morsel-driven pass

memory_detail.lineitem_scan: sequential 6-column parallel scan (~1.92GB total: 4×240MB int32 + 2×480MB double)
memory_detail.index_lookups: random_access_with_prefetch — for each qualifying row, 3 concurrent hash probes; __builtin_prefetch on index entries 8-16 rows ahead
memory_detail.aggregation: hot_flat_array_per_thread — 200 doubles = 1600 bytes, stays in L1 cache throughout

io_strategy.approach: full_column_mmap — no zone map applicable (p_name has no range predicate; lineitem has no range predicate after part filter)
io_strategy.madvise_strategy: MADV_WILLNEED on all lineitem columns + indexes in parallel at startup

execution_notes.nation_name_loading: load 25 lines from nation/n_name_lookup.txt into std::string nation_names[25] at startup
execution_notes.output_sort: sort 175 result entries by (nation_name ASC, o_year DESC) — trivial cost

phase_breakdown.index_lookups_for_3.26M_rows: ~15ms (3 random-access lookups per row, prefetched, 64 threads)
